title: Optimizing React Performance: useMemo and useCallback

questions.sentence: As applications scale, unnecessary re-renders can significantly degrade the performance and user experience.
To mitigate this, React provides optimization hooks like 'useMemo' and 'useCallback'.
'useMemo' is designed to memoize the result of expensive calculations, ensuring they are only re-computed when their dependencies change.
Conversely, 'useCallback' memoizes the function definition itself, preventing child components—especially those wrapped in 'React.memo'—from re-rendering when a parent's state changes.

A senior developer must carefully weigh the performance benefits against the increased complexity, using profiling tools like the React DevTools to identify genuine bottlenecks before implementing these optimizations.
